title: Managing Secrets using kubectl — Edit a Secret
text:
You can edit an existing
Secret
object unless it is
immutable
. To edit a
Secret, run the following command:
kubectl edit secrets <secret-name>
This opens your default editor and allows you to update the base64 encoded
Secret values in the
data
field, such as in the following example:
# Please edit the object below. Lines beginning with a '#' will be ignored,
# and an empty file will abort the edit. If an error occurs while saving this file, it will be
# reopened with the relevant failures.
#
apiVersion
:
v1
data
:
password
:
UyFCXCpkJHpEc2I9
username
:
YWRtaW4=
kind
:
Secret
metadata
:
creationTimestamp
:
"2022-06-28T17:44:13Z"
name
:
db-user-pass
namespace
:
default
resourceVersion
:
"12708504"
uid
:
91becd59-78fa-4c85-823f-6d44436242ac
type
:
Opaque
